items.title: Bitcoin funding rate stays negative even as BTC price trades above $75K: What gives?
items.summary: Bitcoin’s futures funding rate has remained negative even as BTC bounced back above $75,000. Should traders be worried?

items.title: Quantum Proposal Won’t Save Satoshi’s Bitcoin, Says Cardano Founder Hoskinson
items.summary: Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson says "it's not possible" to save all vulnerable Bitcoin from quantum computing under the current proposal.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Key Ethereum researcher Josh Stark leaves the Ethereum Foundation
items.summary: Stark's departure from the Ethereum Foundation is the most high-profile exit from the organization since its shakeup in the first quarter of 2025.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Helps Expose North Korean Workers That Infiltrated Crypto Firms
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation reported that a six-month program helped expose around 100 DPRK IT workers across 53 crypto projects.

items.title: After Kalshi appeal, prediction markets fight could head to US Supreme Court
items.summary: An appellate court is expected to reach a decision after hearing arguments from Kalshi and lawyers representing the state of Nevada.

items.title: OpenAI Super App Takes Shape: Codex Gets Computer Use, Browser, and Image Gen
items.summary: OpenAI's Codex desktop app now controls your Mac, runs its own browser, and generates images. The update pushes Codex further into territory occupied by Claude Code and OpenClaw.

items.title: Russian Crypto Exchange Grinex Halts Trading, Alleging $13M Exploit by 'Western Special Services'
items.summary: Russian crypto exchange Grinex paused trading services after a $13 million exploit it says was conducted by "foreign special services."

items.title: HIVE plans $75M raise to fund AI infrastructure push
items.summary: The Bitcoin miner will use the proceeds to expand GPU capacity and data centers as it scales its high-performance computing business beyond mining.

items.title: Federal AI Use Soars, But Bottlenecks Threaten Momentum Amid Public Skepticism: Brookings
items.summary: The United States government has rapidly adopted AI tools in recent years, but skepticism towards both AI and agencies alike is only growing.

items.title: Suspicious Polymarket Trader Made $320K on Last-Minute 2025 Biden Pardons
items.summary: Two linked wallets flawlessly bet on several pardons made by former President Joe Biden during his final minutes in office.

items.title: Public crypto miners sold more BTC in Q1 2026 than all of 2025: Report
items.summary: Mining companies are now split between those liquidating Bitcoin to cover operating expenses and those holding their BTC in reserve to fuel future growth.

items.title: Your AI Chats Can Be Used Against You in Court—Law Firms Are Scrambling
items.summary: Two months after a New York federal judge ruled AI conversations can be seized by prosecutors, more than a dozen major law firms have issued warnings to clients.

items.title: Bitcoin analysts see ‘further upside,’ and identify next price levels to watch
items.summary: Traders say Bitcoin has room to rally higher, but the upside could be capped at BTC’s active supply cost basis near $78,000.

items.title: With no bipartisan leadership, CFTC won't ‘slow down‘ on rulemaking
items.summary: Democratic lawmakers called out Michael Selig for unilaterally advancing policies at the regulator that's normally led by a bipartisan group of five commissioners.

items.title: Stack BTC CEO Steps Down as Farage-Linked Bitcoin Venture Reshuffles Leadership
items.summary: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage announced his backing of the company following its relaunch under the Stack BTC name in March.

items.title: Claude Opus 4.7 Is Here: Anthropic’s Latest Model Delivers, But It’s a Token Eating Machine
items.summary: Anthropic's new flagship model beat every benchmark we threw at it, eats tokens like a hungry teenager, and showed its reasoning out loud.

items.title: Charles Schwab Weighs Prediction Markets Move as Bitcoin, Ethereum Trading Nears
items.summary: Charles Schwab President and CEO Rick Wurster indicated that America’s largest discount brokerage will likely support prediction markets.
